title: Tender, Gooey Turkish Lamb Tagine
description: A rich, comforting Turkish dish featuring tender lamb, turmeric, and a gooey, flavorful sauce.
instructions: Heat the oil in the bottom of a traditional Turkish clay pot or a heavy Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Brown the lamb pieces on all sides, about 5 minutes, then remove them from the pot and set them aside.
Add the onion to the pot and cook until it's softened and lightly browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic, ginger, turmeric, cumin, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper to the pot and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the flour to the pot and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the browned lamb back to the pot, along with the chicken broth and dried apricots.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then cover the pot and transfer it to the oven.
Braise the lamb for 2 1/2 hours, or until it's tender and the sauce has thickened.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool slightly.
Stir in the Greek yogurt and season the sauce with salt and black pepper to taste.
Serve the lamb and sauce over rice or with some crusty bread, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
